title: Docking / tiling (dockable window-management) — placement of the recursive partition-tree layout model (golden-layout / dockview / FlexLayout / rc-dock family)
summary: Prep research for decision #1437 — the Fork-4 carve-out of #1384 (spatial manipulation / arrangeable surfaces), blockedBy #1384 for the shared a11y + Pointer-Events substrate baseline. #1384 ratified three placements (standalone `resizable` splitter intent we:src/_data/intents/resizable.json, `arrangeable` 2-D grid intent we:src/_data/intents/arrangeable.json, `alignment-guides` intent) over a fixed-mechanic substrate (Pointer Events + setPointerCapture, never HTML DnD; APG Window Splitter / APG Grid a11y; live-region announce; Element.moveBefore progressive) and DEFERRED the recursive partition-tree model here as a genuinely distinct third model. Both shipped intents point at this gap (resizable.json:6, arrangeable.json:32 — 'deferred #1437'). FINDING 1 — the partition-tree is a COMPOSITION of #1384's pieces, not a fourth primitive: it is a recursive tree of two node kinds WE already standardizes — SPLIT nodes (rows/columns whose siblings are separated by a draggable splitter = `resizable` applied recursively) and STACK leaves (tab-stacks = the Tabs pattern WE ships as the `tabs` block, we:src/_data/blocks/tabs.json). It adds NO new gesture and NO new leaf widget; it adds (a) a recursive container contract (node = row|column|stack, nesting arbitrarily, space fully partitioned — no gaps/overlaps ever), (b) drag-to-dock (drop on an edge/center zone to re-tile), (c) tree serialization, (d) optional popout-to-window. Convergent vocabulary across golden-layout/dockview/FlexLayout/rc-dock: row|column → stack|group → tab|component; dockview api.toJSON/fromJSON + FlexLayout Model.fromJson/toJson both serialize this row→column→stack-of-tabs tree. FINDING 2 — popout-to-OS-window is a SEPARATE optional capability, orthogonal to the tiling model (window.open + adoptedStyleSheets clone; the tree is complete without it — FlexLayout/rc-dock tile strongly with weak popout); a generic move-a-live-subtree-to-another-Window capability, not intrinsic to partitioning → recommended out of the dock core, deferred. FINDING 3 — there is NO native APG pattern for docking (the 32-pattern APG set has no docking/tiling/window-manager pattern) but it DOES have the two the model decomposes into: Window Splitter (the splitters, already a fixed invariant on `resizable`) + Tabs (the stacks, already on the `tabs` block). So docking's a11y contract is NOT new — it composes two existing APG contracts; the one docking-specific keyboard surface (drag-to-dock relocation) reuses the #1384 grab/move/drop + live-region mechanic. All four incumbents ship ZERO keyboard/ARIA for docking — the unmet need #1384 Finding 3 reported. FINDING 4 — native-substrate Baseline check: NOTHING new is needed below #1384's baseline (Pointer Events, CSS Grid/Flex partition rendering, Element.moveBefore relocation, window.open+adoptedStyleSheets popout are all Baseline/progressive already in play); there is NO native dock/tab-stack/split-view element and NO Open UI/CSSWG/WHATWG proposal in flight (Popover/Invokers are unrelated). The gap is purely the COMPOSITIONAL model layered over substrate WE already standardizes — which is exactly why the carve is correct AND why it is blockedBy #1384 (the build can't land until `resizable` + the reorder substrate it composes are real). CLASSIFICATION: Intent layer (declarative UX 'what') + composing Block, mirroring #1384's intent+block forced-ratify; not a Project; the serialized tree MIGHT be a Protocol (Fork 2). Composition seam = `dockable` composes `resizable` + `tabs` block + the reorderable-list grab/move/drop substrate, adding no new gesture. Fixed baked mechanics inherited from #1384 (APG Window Splitter + APG Tabs + live-region + Pointer Events + moveBefore; pointer-only is broken). Most-permissive default = opt-in per surface, absent → static layout. Native-first; the serialized layout tree is the single escapable lock surface. FORKS: Fork 1 — placement: own `dockable` intent composing resizable+tabs+arrangeable-substrate (recommended ~85%) vs a configuration/dimension of `arrangeable` (rejected — the recursive no-gaps tree is structurally distinct from arrangeable's flat Array<{x,y,w,h}>, overloading breaks the contract). Fork 2 — serialized layout tree: a first-class WE Protocol/interchange schema (recommended ~70% — it is the one real lock-in surface, an escapable interchange format earns protocol-hood; #239/minimize-lock-in) vs intent-internal persisted state (no protocol). Fork 3 — popout-to-OS-window: an optional `dockable` dimension deferred to the realizing build (recommended ~75%) vs in the core now (rejected — orthogonal, optional, widens the contract) vs its own separate decision. SETTLED (support-by-default): intent+block placement, no project (mirrors #1384 forced-ratify A); the a11y contract is the COMPOSITION of existing APG Window Splitter + Tabs contracts (no new pattern invented); Pointer-Events substrate + never-HTML-DnD + moveBefore relocation inherited as fixed mechanics; the realizing build is deferred (blockedBy #1384). Full grounding in we:reports/2026-06-21-docking-tiling-partition-tree.md.
